sections.title: Talking to Bernina without ghostspeak ability

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo.

sections.title: Talking to Bernina with ghostspeak ability

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Hello? Perhaps you can understand me? If you can hear me that is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, hello there! Yes, I can understand you!

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: You can? You can! How wonderful! It has been a long while since anyone came by that could understand what I wanted to say to them. You wouldn't happen to have a moment to help me out would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could certainly try to help... what did you say your name was again?

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Bernina! I don't think I did mention it. I haven't had to do an introduction for a while now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's lovely to meet you, Bernina. I'm [player name]. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: It's very unfortunate really. I died.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, that explains the incorporeality. What happened?

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: I was a crew member on board a ship sailing out of Fortis. We got caught in a storm and sailed too close to the rocks around Shipwreck Cove. I'm sure you can guess the results...

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: The thing is though, after I died, I was not guided to Mictl. I remained, but in this form. I saw the remains of our ship, and I saw some of those salvagers from the east claiming whatever they wanted.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: One of them took a diamond necklace from my body. It was very precious to me. It should not remain in the hands of a thief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I make no promises, but I'll try to recover it.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. I heard the one that took it being called Jasper by one of his fellow salvagers. He is the one you will need to find.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Have you got any leads on my necklace yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: That salvager, Jasper, hopefully still has it. I'm sure you'll find him with the rest of his people. They live to the east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm an adventurer, and I'm looking for someone. Is your name Jasper?

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: It is... How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a lost item and I think you might be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: I'm... familiar with lost items.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you come across a diamond necklace? One belonging to an unfortunate passenger involved in a nearby shipwreck?

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: You wouldn't happen to be talking about... that necklace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That necklace?

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: A necklace crossed my path a while back, and everything suddenly started to go wrong for me. It was as if I was haunted by some spiteful spirit.

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: I did what I do best and I flogged it to some chap that came wandering through. He seemed very keen to buy it. Luck's been much better since!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This person you sold it to... Any idea where I can find him?

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: He looked like some sort of mountain climber. He said he was setting out on some sort of journey that was very important to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Thanks.

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: Still looking for that necklace? I don't have it any more. I don't want anything to do with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The person you sold it to... Any idea where I can find him?

lines.speaker: Jasper
lines.text: He looked like some sort of mountain climber. He said he was setting out on some sort of journey that was very important to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Have you got any leads on my necklace yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. I found the salvager that took it, but it was then sold on to a mountain climber.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Oh no. I'd hoped he wouldn't sell it on so quickly. Maybe there's still hope though. Any mountain climber around here will surely be heading up the Proudspire...

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: I actually did the climb myself once. I promised my son we'd go up there again together, but we never found the time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure I can find this climber. I'll head up the Proudspire right away.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Hey, iknami. Sorry to be a bother, but could you lend us a hand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, what's up?

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: I'm just having a rotten day. My logs won't light and it's gone cold up here. Could you get a fire going for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Kuani! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Hey, iknami. Any luck with that fire?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have one lit shortly.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Thank you!

sections.title: Talking to Bernard after lighting a fire

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Kuani! This is much nicer now we've got a fire going. The name's Bernard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name]. How's your climb going?

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Worst one I've had. Mum taught me to be well prepared, but I've never had so many issues. I've even worn my sturdier work gear to deal with the creatures out here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is mountaineering in the family?

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Absolutely! In fact, this one's in honour of mum. She always wanted to take us up the Proudspire. I even managed to snag this necklace that reminded me of her just before setting off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You got a necklace from the salvagers? Is it a diamond necklace?

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: That's right... How did you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been looking for one like it. Bernard, was it?

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Yes! Bernard, son of Bernina!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Forgive me, Bernard, but there's someone I need to go and have a word with...

sections.title: Talking to Bernard again after lighting a fire

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Hello again. Hope things are going well with you. Did you speak to that person you needed to talk to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I'd better go and see them now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the climber and your necklace!

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: You did? Were you able to get it back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I don't think I need to. Would you be okay with heading up the mountain yourself? There's someone up there you need to see.

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Alright then. I'll meet you up there.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Ah, hello again, [player name]. I don't want to cause alarm, but there's a ghost here with us!

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: He can't understand me. Please, [player name], tell him who I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No need to panic, Bernard. This isn't just some random ghost. This is your mother.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Mother... By Ralos...

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: Tell him I'm proud of him, and... tell him I'm sorry I never came home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't understand her, but she wants you to know she's proud of you, and that she's sorry she didn't come home.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Oh... Mum...

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: I think I know now... I know why I stayed behind. It was for this. Thank you, [player name]. I think it's time for me to go now. Tell Bernard I will always be with him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She has to go now, but she will always be with you, Bernard.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Goodbye mum... I hope I keep making you proud. This necklace... It's not just like yours is it? It is yours. I'm going to finish this climb, for both of us.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: I'll bury the necklace at the top. That way, even once I'm done, a part of us will always be on top of the world.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. Without you, I'd never have got to say goodbye. Now, I guess I'd better get going. I've got a mountain to climb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Best of luck, Bernard.

lines.speaker: Bernard
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. Without you, I'd never have got to say goodbye. Now, I guess I'd better get going. I've got a mountain to climb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Best of luck, Bernard.
